title: Credit Readiness
subtitle: Lending eligibility score
ask_ai_prompt: My credit readiness score is {total}/{maxTotal} (Grade {grade}). {weakAreas}. What should I improve first to qualify for business loans in {country} ({lenders})?
factor_digital_tip_low: Connect more data sources and maintain consistent digital records to improve this score.
factor_digital_tip_high: Strong digital footprint — lenders can verify your business activity.
factor_revenue_tip_low: Consistent monthly revenue improves lending decisions. Avoid cash-only periods.
factor_revenue_tip_high: Revenue level and consistency look strong for lending.
factor_profit_tip_low: Lenders want to see healthy margins. Focus on COGS reduction and pricing optimization.
factor_profit_tip_high: Healthy margins signal strong ability to service debt.
factor_cash_tip_low: Build a cash reserve of 3-6 months expenses. Reduce stockouts to show operational control.
factor_cash_tip_high: Good cash position and inventory management.
factor_compliance_detail: Based on tax compliance readiness and record-keeping consistency
factor_compliance_tip_low: Register with your tax authority and keep all receipts digital. Lenders check compliance status.
factor_compliance_tip_high: Compliance posture looks solid for lending applications.
hero_grade_low: Needs Improvement — Build digital trail first
hero_desc_high: Your digital records and financial performance are strong enough for {lenders} products.
hero_desc_low: Focus on the areas below to strengthen your profile. {lender} may be available now.
dscr_explainer: DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) measures ability to service debt. Lenders typically require 1.25x+. Estimates based on current net income.
footer_disclaimer: Score is indicative only — actual lending decisions depend on additional factors. {tip}
